mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points within one month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of industrial production and consumer credit indicates a supply-demand imbalance; when INDPRO rises while RSAFS falls, it signals weakening demand against stable supply, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of decelerating real GDP growth, equity valuations contract as earnings revisions turn negative, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 growth decelerates for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding real economic activity and higher corporate throughput, which directly supports equity cash flows and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a threshold of 3.5 percent, persistent inflation expectations force central bank hawkishness, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL breaches the 3.5 percent threshold, or the signal produces false positives in > 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous strength of nonfarm payrolls growth and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and labor market tightness, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series rise concurrently, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a divergence where FEDFUNDS stabilizes while UNRATE declines signals successful monetary transmission and labor market healing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when FEDFUNDS stabilizes and UNRATE falls, or the divergence signal shows no predictive edge over a baseline momentum strategy.
